evals.prompt: Create a KubeSphere OpenPitrix application repository manifest for workspace demo. The repo name is bitnami, display name is Bitnami, URL is https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami, and it should not sync periodically.
evals.expected_output: A valid application.kubesphere.io/v2 Repo YAML with metadata.name bitnami, workspace label demo, display-name annotation Bitnami, spec.url https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami, and spec.syncPeriod 0.

assertions.name: uses_v2_repo
assertions.description: The manifest uses the current KubeSphere application API.
assertions.check: The YAML should include apiVersion application.kubesphere.io/v2 and kind Repo.

assertions.name: workspace_label_set
assertions.description: The repository is scoped to the requested workspace.
assertions.check: metadata.labels should contain kubesphere.io/workspace: demo.

assertions.name: repo_url_set
assertions.description: The repository URL is preserved exactly.
assertions.check: spec.url should be https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami.

assertions.name: no_periodic_sync
assertions.description: Periodic sync is disabled.
assertions.check: spec.syncPeriod should be 0.

evals.prompt: The OpenPitrix repo named bitnami is stuck syncing in KubeSphere. Give me the kubectl commands and checks to diagnose it.
evals.expected_output: A focused troubleshooting sequence that describes the Repo, checks repository events, inspects ks-controller-manager logs for helmrepo-controller and bitnami, validates the repo URL/index.yaml and credentials, and explains common causes.

assertions.name: describes_repo
assertions.description: Includes a command to inspect the Repo resource.
assertions.check: Should include kubectl describe repo.application.kubesphere.io bitnami.

assertions.name: checks_events
assertions.description: Includes a command to inspect events for the repository.
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get events with involvedObject.name=bitnami.

assertions.name: checks_controller_logs
assertions.description: Includes controller log inspection.
assertions.check: Should mention ks-controller-manager logs and helmrepo-controller.

assertions.name: checks_index_and_credentials
assertions.description: Mentions repository URL/index and credential validation.
assertions.check: Should mention index.yaml and credentials or TLS settings.

evals.prompt: I uploaded a Helm app package to workspace demo and now need to verify the generated OpenPitrix app and version. What resources and labels should I query?
evals.expected_output: Commands to query applications.application.kubesphere.io and applicationversions.application.kubesphere.io using repo-name=upload, workspace label demo, and app-id for the generated app. The answer should explain uploaded apps start as draft and versions use ApplicationVersion.

assertions.name: queries_uploaded_apps
assertions.description: Uses the upload repo label for uploaded apps.
assertions.check: Should query applications.application.kubesphere.io with application.kubesphere.io/repo-name=upload.

assertions.name: queries_versions_by_app
assertions.description: Uses the app-id label to find versions.
assertions.check: Should query applicationversions.application.kubesphere.io with application.kubesphere.io/app-id=<app>.

assertions.name: workspace_filter
assertions.description: Keeps the query scoped to the requested workspace.
assertions.check: Should include kubesphere.io/workspace=demo.

assertions.name: draft_state
assertions.description: Explains initial review state for uploads.
assertions.check: Should mention uploaded apps or versions start in draft.

evals.prompt: An OpenPitrix ApplicationRelease named nginx failed to install in namespace demo-ns. Show me how to troubleshoot the release and executor job.
evals.expected_output: Commands to describe the ApplicationRelease, derive or use the target namespace, list Jobs and Pods with application.kubesphere.io/app-release-name=nginx label selectors, fetch pod logs through that selector, and check appVersionID, target cluster/namespace labels, chart storage, and status/specHash.

assertions.name: describes_release
assertions.description: Inspects the ApplicationRelease resource.
assertions.check: Should include kubectl describe applicationrelease.application.kubesphere.io nginx.

assertions.name: checks_executor_job
assertions.description: Finds the Helm executor Job by release label.
assertions.check: Should list jobs in demo-ns with application.kubesphere.io/app-release-name=nginx.

assertions.name: checks_executor_logs
assertions.description: Fetches pod logs for the executor by release label.
assertions.check: Should include kubectl logs for pods selected by application.kubesphere.io/app-release-name=nginx.

assertions.name: avoids_primary_grep
assertions.description: Avoids broad grep as the primary troubleshooting path.
assertions.check: Should prefer kubectl label selectors over kubectl get jobs -A | grep nginx or kubectl get pods -A | grep nginx.

assertions.name: mentions_release_references
assertions.description: Checks app version and target labels.
assertions.check: Should mention spec.appVersionID and kubesphere.io/cluster or kubesphere.io/namespace labels.

evals.prompt: Explain the mapping between old OpenPitrix resources and KubeSphere 4.x application resources. I am looking at HelmRepo, HelmApplication, HelmApplicationVersion, HelmRelease, and HelmCategory.
evals.expected_output: A mapping table or concise explanation: HelmRepo to Repo, HelmApplication to Application, HelmApplicationVersion to ApplicationVersion, HelmRelease to ApplicationRelease, and HelmCategory to Category. It should recommend using application.kubesphere.io/v2 first and mention older openpitrix.io/v2 CRUD routes plus openpitrix.io/v2alpha1 read/list routes as compatibility APIs.

assertions.name: repo_mapping
assertions.description: Maps repository resources.
assertions.check: Should map HelmRepo to Repo.

assertions.name: application_mapping
assertions.description: Maps app template and version resources.
assertions.check: Should map HelmApplication to Application and HelmApplicationVersion to ApplicationVersion.

assertions.name: release_mapping
assertions.description: Maps installed app resources.
assertions.check: Should map HelmRelease to ApplicationRelease.

assertions.name: category_mapping
assertions.description: Maps category resources.
assertions.check: Should map HelmCategory to Category.

assertions.name: prefers_v2
assertions.description: Recommends the current API group.
assertions.check: Should recommend application.kubesphere.io/v2 and mention openpitrix.io/v2 or openpitrix.io/v2alpha1 only for compatibility.

evals.prompt: Show API examples for KSE application management and legacy OpenPitrix. Include repository, app/version, release, and category examples. Include curl examples for the KSE JSON APIs.
evals.expected_output: Examples using kubectl, ks_api.py, and curl with clear tool-selection priority. The answer should use ks_api.py and curl for KubeSphere application.kubesphere.io/v2 routes, explain how curl obtains a bearer token, and include legacy openpitrix.io/v2alpha1 read/list examples plus openpitrix.io/v2 CRUD examples. Curl examples should include Authorization: Bearer token and application/json headers. The answer should distinguish the current KSE API from legacy OpenPitrix APIs and mention snake_case fields for legacy requests.

assertions.name: kse_application_v2_examples
assertions.description: Includes current KSE application API examples.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/application.kubesphere.io/v2/workspaces/<workspace>/repos, apps, versions, applications, categories, or reviews examples.

assertions.name: legacy_v2alpha1_examples
assertions.description: Includes legacy read/list OpenPitrix examples.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/openpitrix.io/v2alpha1/workspaces/<workspace>/repos or apps examples.

assertions.name: legacy_v2_crud_examples
assertions.description: Includes legacy CRUD OpenPitrix examples.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/openpitrix.io/v2/workspaces/<workspace>/repos or apps examples.

assertions.name: uses_ks_api
assertions.description: Uses the KubeSphere API helper for JSON calls.
assertions.check: Should show python ks_api.py GET or POST examples.

assertions.name: tool_selection_priority
assertions.description: Explains when to use kubectl, ks_api.py, and curl.
assertions.check: Should say kubectl is for resource/status troubleshooting, ks_api.py is for JSON KAPIs, and curl is for multipart, downloads, custom headers, or explicit HTTP reproduction.

assertions.name: curl_token_source
assertions.description: Explains how curl gets a bearer token.
assertions.check: Should show TOKEN coming from ~/.kubesphere_token after ks_api.py login or from POST /oauth/token.

assertions.name: curl_examples_with_auth
assertions.description: Provides curl examples when requested.
assertions.check: Should show curl commands with Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN and Content-Type: application/json for KSE JSON APIs.

assertions.name: release_body_shape
assertions.description: ApplicationRelease examples should follow ApplicationReleaseSpec.
assertions.check: For KSE ApplicationRelease examples, should use spec.appID, spec.appVersionID, spec.appType, and values as "" or base64 string. Should not invent spec.name or spec.namespace, and should not use values: {}.

assertions.name: distinguishes_apis
assertions.description: Explains which API is preferred.
assertions.check: Should state application.kubesphere.io/v2 is preferred for KubeSphere 4.x and openpitrix.io paths are legacy or compatibility APIs.

evals.prompt: I need to manage all KubeSphere App Store related features through OpenPitrix. Cover enterprise-space Application Management pages: 应用, 自制应用, 应用模板, 应用仓库; and component-dock App Store Management pages: 应用, 应用分类, 应用审核, 应用仓库, 部署管理. Show the APIs and checks for listing app templates, editing app metadata/category/screenshots, managing categories, reviewing uploaded versions, repositories, deployments, and distinguishing templates from installed app releases.
evals.expected_output: A workflow-oriented answer for App Store management. It should map enterprise-space routes such as /workspaces/<workspace>/deploy, app-templates, and app-repos plus component-dock routes such as apps-manage/store, categories, reviews, repo, and deploy to application.kubesphere.io/v2 APIs; use Application for store templates and ApplicationRelease only for installed apps; include PATCH /workspaces/<workspace>/apps/<app> fields such as categoryName, aliasName, description, icon, attachments, abstraction, and appHome; include attachment multipart curl with bearer auth; include category create/update/delete and review list/action examples; include repo sync examples; and include kubectl checks for Application, ApplicationVersion, Repo, Category, and ApplicationRelease when troubleshooting.

assertions.name: maps_app_store_ui_areas
assertions.description: Connects App Store UI areas to backend APIs.
assertions.check: Should mention apps-manage/store, categories, reviews, repo, or deploy and map them to /apps, /categories, /reviews, /repos, and /applications APIs.

assertions.name: maps_enterprise_workspace_areas
assertions.description: Connects enterprise-space application management areas to backend APIs.
assertions.check: Should mention enterprise-space or workspace pages such as /workspaces/<workspace>/deploy, app-templates, app-repos, 应用, 自制应用, 应用模板, or 应用仓库, and map them to ApplicationRelease, Application, ApplicationVersion, and Repo APIs.

assertions.name: distinguishes_templates_from_releases
assertions.description: Does not confuse app templates with installed app releases.
assertions.check: Should state that App Store templates are Application resources and installed apps are ApplicationRelease resources.

assertions.name: metadata_patch_fields
assertions.description: Explains how App Store metadata is edited.
assertions.check: Should include PATCH /kapis/application.kubesphere.io/v2/workspaces/<workspace>/apps/<app> with categoryName, aliasName, description, icon, attachments, abstraction, or appHome.

assertions.name: attachment_management
assertions.description: Includes screenshot or attachment handling.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/application.kubesphere.io/v2/workspaces/<workspace>/attachments and use curl multipart with Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN.

assertions.name: category_management
assertions.description: Includes category CRUD and deletion caveats.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/application.kubesphere.io/v2/categories, category create/update/delete examples, and mention not deleting kubesphere-app-uncategorized or categories with applications.

assertions.name: review_management
assertions.description: Includes review queue behavior.
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/application.kubesphere.io/v2/reviews, review states such as submitted, and version action examples for passed or rejected.

assertions.name: kse_v2_action_and_label_shape
assertions.description: Avoids legacy action/body shapes in KSE v2 examples.
assertions.check: Should use application.kubesphere.io/repo-name=upload, not uploaded. KSE v2 app/version actions should use state, not action. KSE v2 repo sync should use an empty body or no body, not {"action":"sync"} or {"action":"index"}.

assertions.name: kse_v2_object_body_shape
assertions.description: Uses Kubernetes object-shaped bodies for category and repository examples.
assertions.check: Category examples should use metadata.name, metadata.annotations, and spec.icon. Repo examples should use metadata.name, metadata.labels, metadata.annotations, and spec.url/spec.syncPeriod. Should not use simplified top-level {"name":"...","url":"..."} or {"name":"...","displayName":"..."} bodies for KSE v2.

assertions.name: kubectl_checks
assertions.description: Includes cluster-side checks for store management.
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get applications.application.kubesphere.io, applicationversions.application.kubesphere.io, repos.application.kubesphere.io, categories.application.kubesphere.io, or applicationreleases.application.kubesphere.io depending on the workflow.

assertions.name: kubectl_scope
assertions.description: Uses label-scoped CRD queries instead of namespace-scoping application CRDs.
assertions.check: For application CRD checks, should prefer label selectors such as kubesphere.io/workspace, application.kubesphere.io/repo-name, application.kubesphere.io/app-id, or application.kubesphere.io/app-release-name. Should not default to kubectl get applications/applicationversions/repos/categories -n extension-openpitrix.
